Details:
One study suggests ultraviolet irradiation and hydrogen peroxide with iron as catalyst (i.e., a photo-Fenton process) can be effective for alachlor degradation following relatively long periods of exposure compared to typical water treatment technologies. Up to 50% degradation of alachlor was reached in approximately 10 hours. The presence of titanium dioxide increased the degradation to 65% with no increase in reaction time.
Nearly 100% degradation can be achieved in 30 minutes at very high doses (340 mg/L) of hydrogen peroxide in an acidic medium (pH=2.8).  These conditions are not typical of drinking water treatment.
